{{draft}} {{Promising draft}}
[[File:140720_Noda-jinja_Yamaguchi_Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The entrance to the compound where both shrines are located.]]
{{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} are two [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located next to each other in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourism Information Website Nishinokyo Yamaguchi - A strategic base of the Meiji Restoration. - Noda Shrine|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/w/en/ishin/spot018.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref> Both shrines are dedicated to members of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en}}.
== Toyosaka Shrine == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyosaka Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Toyosaka Shrine bridge and entrance.jpg|deity=[[Mōri Motonari]]|consecration_year=1762| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Toyosaka Shrine | image = [[File:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-1 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Toyosaka Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = Mōri Motonari | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1762 (Hōreki 12) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = October 1 | Main festival = Goshōsai (July 16) }} Toyosaka Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi, located next to Noda Shrine.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourist Information {{!}} Toyosaka-jinjya Shinto Shrine {{!}}30{{!}}|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/signqr/details/en/30.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref>[[File:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Motonari, who was a powerful feudal lord in the [[Sengoku period]] and laid the foundation for the Mōri clan of the [[Chōshū Domain]].
=== History === In 1600 (Keichō 5), following the defeat in the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], Mōri Terumoto moved from [[Aki Province]] to [[Nagato Province]]. He enshrined his grandfather Motonari's spirit at Kasuga Shrine in the village of Ekimukai, Hagi. In 1762 (Hōreki 12), the Chōshū domain lord Mōri Shigetoshi rebuilt the local shrine in the Ni-no-Maru of [[Hagi Castle]] and moved Motonari's spirit there, naming it Shōrei Shrine. In 1770 (Meiwa 7), the shrine was renamed Gyōtoku Shrine and Motonari was enshrined as Gyōtoku Daimyōjin.
In 1869 (Meiji 2), Emperor Meiji granted Motonari the divine title of "Toyosaka" due to his reverence for the imperial court. The shrine was moved to the precincts of Taga Shrine in Yamaguchi City and renamed Toyosaka Shrine. The current shrine buildings were constructed in 1872 (Meiji 4), and the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9). In 1882 (Meiji 15), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main shrine building is a three-bay nagare-zukuri style structure. It has a cross-shaped flat layout with an extended roof at the front. In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270

In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Toyosaka Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Noda Shrine}} * [[Shizuki-yama Shrine]]

revision.comment: 54 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:豊榮神社・野田神社 up to rev 102012024
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|参道入口]] '''豊榮神社'''（とよさかじんじゃ）と'''野田神社'''（のだじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]に隣接して鎮座する[[神社]]である。両神社は東に豊榮神社、西に野田神社と隣接するものの別の[[宗教法人]]であり、本殿や拝殿、廻廊等の社殿もそれぞれ別にあるが、社務所を始めとする共有の施設もあり、密接な関係にもあることから、ここでは併せて説明する。 ともに[[旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に指定され、隣接地には[[山口県神社庁]]もある。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 {{座標一覧}} == 豊榮神社 == {{神社 |名称 = 豊榮神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[山口市]]天花1丁目1-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=豊榮神社}} |祭神 = 贈正一位[[毛利元就]]公 |社格 = 旧[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = [[宝暦]]12年（[[1762年]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月1日]] |神事 = 御正祭（7月16日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=豊榮神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|絹本著色毛利元就像]] === 祭神 === [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に[[中国地方]]に覇を唱え後の[[長州藩]][[毛利家]]の礎を築いた[[毛利元就]]を「'''毛利元就公'''」として祀る。 === 歴史 === [[慶長]]5年（[[1600年]]）の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]の敗戦により、同年[[安芸国]]から[[長門国]]に[[移封]]された[[毛利輝元]]が、[[萩市|萩]]江向村の春日神社境内に祖父元就の霊（元就公）を祀ったのが前身で、同12年には春日神社は[[萩城]]の[[三の丸]]に遷座されたが、[[宝暦]]12年（[[1762年]]）、長州藩主[[毛利重就|重就]]が萩城[[二の丸]]に鎮座していた土地神社を改造して春日神社から元就公を遷座し、毛利家の[[祖神]]とされる[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]毛利家は[[大江氏]]（[[土師氏]]）の流れというが、同氏の氏祖が天穂日命である。と合祀して'''神性霊社'''と号したのが直接的な創祀である。[[明和]]7年（[[1770年]]）には元就公を'''仰徳大明神'''と称して改めて主祭神とし、神社名も'''仰徳社'''と称した。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）、元就には[[正親町天皇]]の[[即位]]を援助するなど朝廷を崇敬する事跡があったことから、[[明治天皇]]は[[弁事]][[五辻安仲]]を[[宣命使]]として差遣して祭神（元就公）に「豊栄」の[[神号]]を与え、山口市の[[多賀神社 (山口市)|多賀神社]]境内に仮殿を構えて仰徳社を移祠し'''豊榮神社'''と改称した。同4年、[[岩倉具視]]が[[勅使]]として差遣されて剣1口を奉納し、同年現在地に現社殿が造営されて遷座、同6年に[[県社]]に列した後、同15年（[[1882年]]）には別格官幣社に列格した。 === 社殿・境内 === 本殿は[[流造|三間社流造]]。前方に梁間1間桁行5間の釣屋を建てる。釣屋は[[切妻造]]であるが奥寄り（本殿側）2間目は左右に1間の張り出しを設けて十字型の平面構成とし、前面は幣殿に接続する。幣殿は桁行3間梁間1間の[[入母屋造]]平入、左右からは廻廊が伸びる。幣殿の前方に方3間、入母屋造妻入の舞殿風の拝殿が建ち、その前方に神門を構える。以上、屋根は全て銅板葺。 また、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に境内に「百万一心」の碑が建てられた。毛利元就が[[吉田郡山城]]を増築する際に[[人柱]]に代えて「[[百万一心]]」と彫った石を埋めたという逸話に因むもので、その石は実際に郡山城廃城後に城跡から発見されており、境内絵馬殿には豊榮神社に奉納された石の拓本の額が展示されている。 === 文化財 === （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） ;重要文化財（国指定） *絹本著色毛利元就像1幅（絵画、昭和44年（1969年）6月20日） :縦97[[センチメートル|センチ]]、横49.8センチの掛幅装で、[[大紋]]、[[侍烏帽子]]を著して扇を持ち、合口を差した元就の座像。上部に[[京都]][[南禅寺]]の[[僧]]、[[仁如集堯]]による[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）の[[画賛|賛]]が記され、それによると元就の長子である[[毛利隆元|隆元]]が66歳の父を描かせた[[寿像]]である事が判る。当時の元就は将に中国地方10箇国を併呑しようとする時で、気魄ある老将の姿をよく描いたものとされる。また、元就像は数点あるが寿像はこれのみであり、確実な[[室町時代]]の寿像としても価値の高いものであることから重要文化財の指定を受けた[http://bunkazai.ysn21.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=40009&pid=bl 山口県教育庁社会教育･文化財課「山口県の文化財 絹本著色毛利元就像]（平成23年9月24日閲覧）。。[[山口県立山口博物館|県立山口博物館]]に寄託されている。 == 野田神社 == {{神社 |名称 = 野田神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[山口市]]天花1丁目1-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=野田神社}} |祭神 = [[毛利敬親]]公 |社格 = 旧[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[3月5日]] |神事 = 御正祭（5月17日（主祭神）・12月23日（配祀神）） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=野田神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|能楽堂]] === 祭神 === 長州藩最後の藩主で[[明治維新]]の功労者である[[毛利敬親]]を「'''毛利敬親公'''」として祀り、敬親の養嗣子の[[毛利元徳|元徳]]を「'''毛利元徳公'''」として配祀する。 === 歴史 === 明治6年（[[1873年]]）、有志が謀って「敬親公」を祀る社を豊榮神社の境内に建て、神社名を敬親の[[諡号]]である「忠正」から取って'''忠正神社'''と称したのに創まる。翌7年、鎮座地名からとった現在の'''野田神社'''に改称して同9年に県社に列格、同19年に現在地に遷座し、[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）に別格官幣社に列格した。 元徳は死の2年後の明治31年（[[1898年]]）、野田神社境内に[[摂社]]として'''芳宜園神社'''を創祀して毛利元徳公として祀られたが、[[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）に本殿に[[合祀]]された。 === 社殿・境内 === 本殿、釣屋、幣殿、拝殿等、社殿の結構はほぼ豊榮神社に同じ。 境内の[[能楽堂]]は毛利元徳公として配祀される元徳の長男である[[毛利元昭|元昭]]により昭和11年に新築寄進されたもので、[[平成]]3年（[[1991年]]）に山口市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 === 文化財 === （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） ;重要文化財（国指定） *太刀 銘□友（伝・助友）附 衛府太刀拵（工芸品、大正元年（1912年）9月3日） :長さ63.5センチ、反り 1.9センチの[[鎬造]]の[[太刀]]。銘は「友」の上の一字が判読不能であるが[[平安時代]]の助友の作と伝えられ、その様態から武器としてではなく儀式用の太刀と推定される。刀身は反りの中心点が元寄り（腰の辺り）にある腰反りで、踏張りも見られる。大正元年に当時の[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく国宝（[[旧国宝]]）に指定され、昭和25年（[[1950年]]）8月29日の[[文化財保護法]]施行に伴い重要文化財とされた。附（つけたり）指定の拵（こしらえ）は、柄（つか）を白鮫着せとし、鞘は金梨子地の[[蒔絵]]に[[螺鈿]]細工で紋を表し、上品かつ華やかな美しさを持つ[http://bunkazai.ysn21.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=70064&pid=bl 山口県教育庁社会教育･文化財課「山口県の文化財 太刀 銘□友（伝・助友）]（平成23年9月24日閲覧）。。豊榮神社所蔵の元就像同様山口博物館に寄託されている。 ;山口市指定有形文化財 *野田神社能楽堂及び付属室（建造物）、平成3年4月23日） :毛利元昭が[[明治維新]]70年記念として昭和11年に寄進したもの。総[[檜]]造りで、[[能楽#能舞台の構造|橋掛（はしがかり）]]、[[能楽#能舞台の構造|鏡の間]]及び[[楽屋]]、控の間を備えた本格的な能楽堂で、[[西日本]]に数少ない能楽堂の中でも規模と質とにおいて群を抜くとされる事から市の文化財に指定された[http://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunka/a3.html 山口市教育委員会「野田神社能楽堂及び付属室」]（平成23年9月24日閲覧）。。当初は現在地より西南の[[野田学園]]の運動場内に建てられていたが、平成3年に移築された。設計、建設は[[東京]]の小林建築事務所が担当。 == 交通 == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[山口線]][[上山口駅]]より徒歩15分ほど。 == 周辺 == * [[野田学園中学校・高等学校]] * [[山口市菜香亭]] * [[雲谷庵]]跡 * [[瑠璃光寺]] * [[洞春寺]]、のむら美術館 * [[常栄寺 (山口市)|常栄寺]] * [[山口県護国神社]] * [[今八幡宮]] == 備考 == === 大田市の豊栄神社 === [[島根県]][[大田市]]の[[石見銀山]]の近くにも、毛利元就を祭神とする'''豊栄神社'''がある。 毛利元就自身が自らの姿を彫ったものとされる木像を[[神体]]としている。元々は[[曹洞宗]]の'''長安寺'''という寺院であり、一説には、[[元亀]]2年（[[1571年]]）に毛利輝元が建立したという。[[慶応]]2年（[[1866年]]）、長州軍が石見に進駐した際、長安寺に元就の木像が祀られているのに驚いたという。翌慶応3年、長州軍の隊士により長安寺境内に霊社が造営された。明治3年、前年に明治天皇から与えられた神号をつけて豊栄神社とし、長安寺は廃寺となった。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 参照 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』38〜41頁、92頁〜95頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] *『神道大辞典』第3巻、平凡社、昭和15年 * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』神社新報社、1968年、43、46頁 * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、252、269-270頁 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|豊榮神社}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|野田神社}} *[[志都岐山神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://yamaguchi-city.jp/details/ab_toyosaka.html 豊栄神社・野田神社] - 山口観光コンベンション協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:とよさかしんしやのたしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:山口市の重要文化財]] [[Category:安芸毛利氏|社とよさかしんしや]] [[Category:長州藩]] [[Category:毛利元就]] [[Category:毛利輝元]] [[Category:毛利敬親|のたしんしや]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[豊榮神社・野田神社]] to [[Toyosaka and Noda Shrines]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|参道入口]] '''豊榮神社'''（とよさかじんじゃ）と'''野田神社'''（のだじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]に隣接して鎮座する[[神社]]である。両神社は東に豊榮神社、西に野田神社と隣接するものの別の[[宗教法人]]であり、本殿や拝殿、廻廊等の社殿もそれぞれ別にあるが、社務所を始めとする共有の施設もあり、密接な関係にもあることから、ここでは併せて説明する。 ともに[[旧社格]]は[[別格官幣社]]で現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に指定され、隣接地には[[山口県神社庁]]もある。[[江戸時代]]後期から[[明治時代]]初期に流行した[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 {{座標一覧}} == 豊榮神社 == {{神社 |名称 = 豊榮神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[山口市]]天花1丁目1-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=豊榮神社}} |祭神 = 贈正一位[[毛利元就]]公 |社格 = 旧[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = [[宝暦]]12年（[[1762年]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月1日]] |神事 = 御正祭（7月16日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=豊榮神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|絹本著色毛利元就像]] === 祭神 === [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]に[[中国地方]]に覇を唱え後の[[長州藩]][[毛利家]]の礎を築いた[[毛利元就]]を「'''毛利元就公'''」として祀る。 === 歴史 === [[慶長]]5年（[[1600年]]）の[[関ヶ原の戦い]]の敗戦により、同年[[安芸国]]から[[長門国]]に[[移封]]された[[毛利輝元]]が、[[萩市|萩]]江向村の春日神社境内に祖父元就の霊（元就公）を祀ったのが前身で、同12年には春日神社は[[萩城]]の[[三の丸]]に遷座されたが、[[宝暦]]12年（[[1762年]]）、長州藩主[[毛利重就|重就]]が萩城[[二の丸]]に鎮座していた土地神社を改造して春日神社から元就公を遷座し、毛利家の[[祖神]]とされる[[アメノホヒ|天穂日命]]毛利家は[[大江氏]]（[[土師氏]]）の流れというが、同氏の氏祖が天穂日命である。と合祀して'''神性霊社'''と号したのが直接的な創祀である。[[明和]]7年（[[1770年]]）には元就公を'''仰徳大明神'''と称して改めて主祭神とし、神社名も'''仰徳社'''と称した。 [[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）、元就には[[正親町天皇]]の[[即位]]を援助するなど朝廷を崇敬する事跡があったことから、[[明治天皇]]は[[弁事]][[五辻安仲]]を[[宣命使]]として差遣して祭神（元就公）に「豊栄」の[[神号]]を与え、山口市の[[多賀神社 (山口市)|多賀神社]]境内に仮殿を構えて仰徳社を移祠し'''豊榮神社'''と改称した。同4年、[[岩倉具視]]が[[勅使]]として差遣されて剣1口を奉納し、同年現在地に現社殿が造営されて遷座、同6年に[[県社]]に列した後、同15年（[[1882年]]）には別格官幣社に列格した。 === 社殿・境内 === 本殿は[[流造|三間社流造]]。前方に梁間1間桁行5間の釣屋を建てる。釣屋は[[切妻造]]であるが奥寄り（本殿側）2間目は左右に1間の張り出しを設けて十字型の平面構成とし、前面は幣殿に接続する。幣殿は桁行3間梁間1間の[[入母屋造]]平入、左右からは廻廊が伸びる。幣殿の前方に方3間、入母屋造妻入の舞殿風の拝殿が建ち、その前方に神門を構える。以上、屋根は全て銅板葺。 また、[[平成]]9年（[[1997年]]）に境内に「百万一心」の碑が建てられた。毛利元就が[[吉田郡山城]]を増築する際に[[人柱]]に代えて「[[百万一心]]」と彫った石を埋めたという逸話に因むもので、その石は実際に郡山城廃城後に城跡から発見されており、境内絵馬殿には豊榮神社に奉納された石の拓本の額が展示されている。 === 文化財 === （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） ;重要文化財（国指定） *絹本著色毛利元就像1幅（絵画、昭和44年（1969年）6月20日） :縦97[[センチメートル|センチ]]、横49.8センチの掛幅装で、[[大紋]]、[[侍烏帽子]]を著して扇を持ち、合口を差した元就の座像。上部に[[京都]][[南禅寺]]の[[僧]]、[[仁如集堯]]による[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）の[[画賛|賛]]が記され、それによると元就の長子である[[毛利隆元|隆元]]が66歳の父を描かせた[[寿像]]である事が判る。当時の元就は将に中国地方10箇国を併呑しようとする時で、気魄ある老将の姿をよく描いたものとされる。また、元就像は数点あるが寿像はこれのみであり、確実な[[室町時代]]の寿像としても価値の高いものであることから重要文化財の指定を受けた[http://bunkazai.ysn21.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=40009&pid=bl 山口県教育庁社会教育･文化財課「山口県の文化財 絹本著色毛利元就像]（平成23年9月24日閲覧）。。[[山口県立山口博物館|県立山口博物館]]に寄託されている。 == 野田神社 == {{神社 |名称 = 野田神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[山口県]][[山口市]]天花1丁目1-2 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=野田神社}} |祭神 = [[毛利敬親]]公 |社格 = 旧[[別格官幣社]]・[[別表神社]] |創建 = [[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]） |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[3月5日]] |神事 = 御正祭（5月17日（主祭神）・12月23日（配祀神）） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Yamaguchi|label=野田神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|能楽堂]] === 祭神 === 長州藩最後の藩主で[[明治維新]]の功労者である[[毛利敬親]]を「'''毛利敬親公'''」として祀り、敬親の養嗣子の[[毛利元徳|元徳]]を「'''毛利元徳公'''」として配祀する。 === 歴史 === 明治6年（[[1873年]]）、有志が謀って「敬親公」を祀る社を豊榮神社の境内に建て、神社名を敬親の[[諡号]]である「忠正」から取って'''忠正神社'''と称したのに創まる。翌7年、鎮座地名からとった現在の'''野田神社'''に改称して同9年に県社に列格、同19年に現在地に遷座し、[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）に別格官幣社に列格した。 元徳は死の2年後の明治31年（[[1898年]]）、野田神社境内に[[摂社]]として'''芳宜園神社'''を創祀して毛利元徳公として祀られたが、[[昭和]]11年（[[1936年]]）に本殿に[[合祀]]された。 === 社殿・境内 === 本殿、釣屋、幣殿、拝殿等、社殿の結構はほぼ豊榮神社に同じ。 境内の[[能楽堂]]は毛利元徳公として配祀される元徳の長男である[[毛利元昭|元昭]]により昭和11年に新築寄進されたもので、[[平成]]3年（[[1991年]]）に山口市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 === 文化財 === （件名後の括弧内は指定の種別と年月日） ;重要文化財（国指定） *太刀 銘□友（伝・助友）附 衛府太刀拵（工芸品、大正元年（1912年）9月3日） :長さ63.5センチ、反り 1.9センチの[[鎬造]]の[[太刀]]。銘は「友」の上の一字が判読不能であるが[[平安時代]]の助友の作と伝えられ、その様態から武器としてではなく儀式用の太刀と推定される。刀身は反りの中心点が元寄り（腰の辺り）にある腰反りで、踏張りも見られる。大正元年に当時の[[古社寺保存法]]に基づく国宝（[[旧国宝]]）に指定され、昭和25年（[[1950年]]）8月29日の[[文化財保護法]]施行に伴い重要文化財とされた。附（つけたり）指定の拵（こしらえ）は、柄（つか）を白鮫着せとし、鞘は金梨子地の[[蒔絵]]に[[螺鈿]]細工で紋を表し、上品かつ華やかな美しさを持つ[http://bunkazai.ysn21.jp/bunkazai/detail.asp?mid=70064&pid=bl 山口県教育庁社会教育･文化財課「山口県の文化財 太刀 銘□友（伝・助友）]（平成23年9月24日閲覧）。。豊榮神社所蔵の元就像同様山口博物館に寄託されている。 ;山口市指定有形文化財 *野田神社能楽堂及び付属室（建造物）、平成3年4月23日） :毛利元昭が[[明治維新]]70年記念として昭和11年に寄進したもの。総[[檜]]造りで、[[能楽#能舞台の構造|橋掛（はしがかり）]]、[[能楽#能舞台の構造|鏡の間]]及び[[楽屋]]、控の間を備えた本格的な能楽堂で、[[西日本]]に数少ない能楽堂の中でも規模と質とにおいて群を抜くとされる事から市の文化財に指定された[http://www.city.yamaguchi.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunka/a3.html 山口市教育委員会「野田神社能楽堂及び付属室」]（平成23年9月24日閲覧）。。当初は現在地より西南の[[野田学園]]の運動場内に建てられていたが、平成3年に移築された。設計、建設は[[東京]]の小林建築事務所が担当。 == 交通 == *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[山口線]][[上山口駅]]より徒歩15分ほど。 == 周辺 == * [[野田学園中学校・高等学校]] * [[山口市菜香亭]] * [[雲谷庵]]跡 * [[瑠璃光寺]] * [[洞春寺]]、のむら美術館 * [[常栄寺 (山口市)|常栄寺]] * [[山口県護国神社]] * [[今八幡宮]] == 備考 == === 大田市の豊栄神社 === [[島根県]][[大田市]]の[[石見銀山]]の近くにも、毛利元就を祭神とする'''豊栄神社'''がある。 毛利元就自身が自らの姿を彫ったものとされる木像を[[神体]]としている。元々は[[曹洞宗]]の'''長安寺'''という寺院であり、一説には、[[元亀]]2年（[[1571年]]）に毛利輝元が建立したという。[[慶応]]2年（[[1866年]]）、長州軍が石見に進駐した際、長安寺に元就の木像が祀られているのに驚いたという。翌慶応3年、長州軍の隊士により長安寺境内に霊社が造営された。明治3年、前年に明治天皇から与えられた神号をつけて豊栄神社とし、長安寺は廃寺となった。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 参照 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 二六興信所編纂　山田米吉編『勤王事蹟別格官幣社精史』38〜41頁、92頁〜95頁　二六興信所　1935年（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）] *『神道大辞典』第3巻、平凡社、昭和15年 * 安津素彦・梅田義彦編集兼監修者『神道辞典』神社新報社、1968年、43、46頁 * 白井永二・土岐昌訓編集『神社辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1979年、252、269-270頁 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|豊榮神社}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|野田神社}} *[[志都岐山神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://yamaguchi-city.jp/details/ab_toyosaka.html 豊栄神社・野田神社] - 山口観光コンベンション協会 {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:とよさかしんしやのたしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:大田市の歴史]] [[category:大田市の建築物]] [[Category:別格官幣社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:山口市の重要文化財]] [[Category:安芸毛利氏|社とよさかしんしや]] While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Toyosaka and Noda Shrines]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{draft}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]]<!--all this actually mandates is a completion tier 7 ranking. We are not using the promising draft template here. If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever-->
[[File:140720_Noda-jinja_Yamaguchi_Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The entrance to the compound where both shrines are located.]]
{{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} are two [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located next to each other in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourism Information Website Nishinokyo Yamaguchi - A strategic base of the Meiji Restoration. - Noda Shrine|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/w/en/ishin/spot018.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref> Both shrines are dedicated to members of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en}}.
== Toyosaka Shrine == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyosaka Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Toyosaka Shrine bridge and entrance.jpg|deity=[[Mōri Motonari]]|consecration_year=1762| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Toyosaka Shrine | image = [[File:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-1 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Toyosaka Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = Mōri Motonari | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1762 (Hōreki 12) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = October 1 | Main festival = Goshōsai (July 16) }} Toyosaka Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi, located next to Noda Shrine.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourist Information {{!}} Toyosaka-jinjya Shinto Shrine {{!}}30{{!}}|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/signqr/details/en/30.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref>[[File:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Motonari, who was a powerful feudal lord in the [[Sengoku period]] and laid the foundation for the Mōri clan of the [[Chōshū Domain]].
=== History === In 1600 (Keichō 5), following the defeat in the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], Mōri Terumoto moved from [[Aki Province]] to [[Nagato Province]]. He enshrined his grandfather Motonari's spirit at Kasuga Shrine in the village of Ekimukai, Hagi. In 1762 (Hōreki 12), the Chōshū domain lord Mōri Shigetoshi rebuilt the local shrine in the Ni-no-Maru of [[Hagi Castle]] and moved Motonari's spirit there, naming it Shōrei Shrine. In 1770 (Meiwa 7), the shrine was renamed Gyōtoku Shrine and Motonari was enshrined as Gyōtoku Daimyōjin.
In 1869 (Meiji 2), Emperor Meiji granted Motonari the divine title of "Toyosaka" due to his reverence for the imperial court. The shrine was moved to the precincts of Taga Shrine in Yamaguchi City and renamed Toyosaka Shrine. The current shrine buildings were constructed in 1872 (Meiji 4), and the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9). In 1882 (Meiji 15), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main shrine building is a three-bay nagare-zukuri style structure. It has a cross-shaped flat layout with an extended roof at the front. In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Toyosaka Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Noda Shrine}} * [[Shizuki-yama Shrine]]

[[File:140720_Noda-jinja_Yamaguchi_Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The entrance to the compound where both shrines are located.]]
{{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} are two [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located next to each other in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourism Information Website Nishinokyo Yamaguchi - A strategic base of the Meiji Restoration. - Noda Shrine|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/w/en/ishin/spot018.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref> Both shrines are dedicated to members of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en}}.
== Toyosaka Shrine == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyosaka Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Toyosaka Shrine bridge and entrance.jpg|deity=[[Mōri Motonari]]|consecration_year=1762| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Toyosaka Shrine | image = [[File:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-1 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Toyosaka Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = Mōri Motonari | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1762 (Hōreki 12) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = October 1 | Main festival = Goshōsai (July 16) }} Toyosaka Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi, located next to Noda Shrine.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourist Information {{!}} Toyosaka-jinjya Shinto Shrine {{!}}30{{!}}|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/signqr/details/en/30.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref>[[File:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Motonari, who was a powerful feudal lord in the [[Sengoku period]] and laid the foundation for the Mōri clan of the [[Chōshū Domain]].
=== History === In 1600 (Keichō 5), following the defeat in the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], Mōri Terumoto moved from [[Aki Province]] to [[Nagato Province]]. He enshrined his grandfather Motonari's spirit at Kasuga Shrine in the village of Ekimukai, Hagi. In 1762 (Hōreki 12), the Chōshū domain lord Mōri Shigetoshi rebuilt the local shrine in the Ni-no-Maru of [[Hagi Castle]] and moved Motonari's spirit there, naming it Shōrei Shrine. In 1770 (Meiwa 7), the shrine was renamed Gyōtoku Shrine and Motonari was enshrined as Gyōtoku Daimyōjin.
In 1869 (Meiji 2), Emperor Meiji granted Motonari the divine title of "Toyosaka" due to his reverence for the imperial court. The shrine was moved to the precincts of Taga Shrine in Yamaguchi City and renamed Toyosaka Shrine. The current shrine buildings were constructed in 1872 (Meiji 4), and the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9). In 1882 (Meiji 15), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main shrine building is a three-bay nagare-zukuri style structure. It has a cross-shaped flat layout with an extended roof at the front. In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270

[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.

{{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Toyosaka and Noda Shrines]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Toyosaka and Noda Shrines]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:35, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{draft}}
[[File:140720_Noda-jinja_Yamaguchi_Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The entrance to the compound where both shrines are located.]]
{{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} are two [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located next to each other in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourism Information Website Nishinokyo Yamaguchi - A strategic base of the Meiji Restoration. - Noda Shrine|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/w/en/ishin/spot018.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref> Both shrines are dedicated to members of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en}}.
== Toyosaka Shrine == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyosaka Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Toyosaka Shrine bridge and entrance.jpg|deity=[[Mōri Motonari]]|consecration_year=1762| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Toyosaka Shrine | image = [[File:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-1 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Toyosaka Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = Mōri Motonari | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1762 (Hōreki 12) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = October 1 | Main festival = Goshōsai (July 16) }} Toyosaka Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi, located next to Noda Shrine.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourist Information {{!}} Toyosaka-jinjya Shinto Shrine {{!}}30{{!}}|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/signqr/details/en/30.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref>[[File:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Motonari, who was a powerful feudal lord in the [[Sengoku period]] and laid the foundation for the Mōri clan of the [[Chōshū Domain]].
=== History === In 1600 (Keichō 5), following the defeat in the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], Mōri Terumoto moved from [[Aki Province]] to [[Nagato Province]]. He enshrined his grandfather Motonari's spirit at Kasuga Shrine in the village of Ekimukai, Hagi. In 1762 (Hōreki 12), the Chōshū domain lord Mōri Shigetoshi rebuilt the local shrine in the Ni-no-Maru of [[Hagi Castle]] and moved Motonari's spirit there, naming it Shōrei Shrine. In 1770 (Meiwa 7), the shrine was renamed Gyōtoku Shrine and Motonari was enshrined as Gyōtoku Daimyōjin.
In 1869 (Meiji 2), Emperor Meiji granted Motonari the divine title of "Toyosaka" due to his reverence for the imperial court. The shrine was moved to the precincts of Taga Shrine in Yamaguchi City and renamed Toyosaka Shrine. The current shrine buildings were constructed in 1872 (Meiji 4), and the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9). In 1882 (Meiji 15), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main shrine building is a three-bay nagare-zukuri style structure. It has a cross-shaped flat layout with an extended roof at the front. In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Toyosaka Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Noda Shrine}} * [[Shizuki-yama Shrine]]
{{Shinto-stub}}

The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].

In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.

The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. {{draft}}
[[File:140720_Noda-jinja_Yamaguchi_Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The entrance to the compound where both shrines are located.]]
{{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} are two [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located next to each other in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourism Information Website Nishinokyo Yamaguchi - A strategic base of the Meiji Restoration. - Noda Shrine|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/w/en/ishin/spot018.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref> Both shrines are dedicated to members of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en}}.
== Toyosaka Shrine == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyosaka Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Toyosaka Shrine bridge and entrance.jpg|deity=[[Mōri Motonari]]|consecration_year=1762| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Toyosaka Shrine | image = [[File:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-1 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Toyosaka Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = Mōri Motonari | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1762 (Hōreki 12) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = October 1 | Main festival = Goshōsai (July 16) }} Toyosaka Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi, located next to Noda Shrine.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourist Information {{!}} Toyosaka-jinjya Shinto Shrine {{!}}30{{!}}|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/signqr/details/en/30.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref>[[File:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Motonari, who was a powerful feudal lord in the [[Sengoku period]] and laid the foundation for the Mōri clan of the [[Chōshū Domain]].
=== History === In 1600 (Keichō 5), following the defeat in the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], Mōri Terumoto moved from [[Aki Province]] to [[Nagato Province]]. He enshrined his grandfather Motonari's spirit at Kasuga Shrine in the village of Ekimukai, Hagi. In 1762 (Hōreki 12), the Chōshū domain lord Mōri Shigetoshi rebuilt the local shrine in the Ni-no-Maru of [[Hagi Castle]] and moved Motonari's spirit there, naming it Shōrei Shrine. In 1770 (Meiwa 7), the shrine was renamed Gyōtoku Shrine and Motonari was enshrined as Gyōtoku Daimyōjin.
In 1869 (Meiji 2), Emperor Meiji granted Motonari the divine title of "Toyosaka" due to his reverence for the imperial court. The shrine was moved to the precincts of Taga Shrine in Yamaguchi City and renamed Toyosaka Shrine. The current shrine buildings were constructed in 1872 (Meiji 4), and the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9). In 1882 (Meiji 15), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main shrine building is a three-bay nagare-zukuri style structure. It has a cross-shaped flat layout with an extended roof at the front. In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Toyosaka Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Noda Shrine}} * [[Shizuki-yama Shrine]]
{{Shinto-stub}}

{{afc comment|1=This page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then deleted but preserved on everybodywiki. The imported history from everybodywiki is patchwork and contains all the contributing users, but not all the revisions are actually present. Lets hope this is sufficient attribution though especially since most of them are mine.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 2 May 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=see for the history[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:26, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Toyosaka and Noda Shrines]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[File:140720_Noda-jinja_Yamaguchi_Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The entrance to the compound where both shrines are located.]]
{{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} are two [[Shinto shrine|Shinto shrines]] located next to each other in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourism Information Website Nishinokyo Yamaguchi - A strategic base of the Meiji Restoration. - Noda Shrine|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/w/en/ishin/spot018.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref> Both shrines are dedicated to members of the {{ill|Mōri clan|en}}.
== Toyosaka Shrine == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Toyosaka Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Toyosaka Shrine bridge and entrance.jpg|deity=[[Mōri Motonari]]|consecration_year=1762| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Toyosaka Shrine | image = [[File:140720 Toyosaka-jinja Yamaguchi Japan02s3.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-1 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|19.7|N|131|28|50.8|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Toyosaka Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | deity = Mōri Motonari | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1762 (Hōreki 12) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = October 1 | Main festival = Goshōsai (July 16) }} Toyosaka Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Yamaguchi, located next to Noda Shrine.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Yamaguchi City Tourist Information {{!}} Toyosaka-jinjya Shinto Shrine {{!}}30{{!}}|url=https://yamaguchi-city.jp/signqr/details/en/30.html|access-date=2023-04-07|website=yamaguchi-city.jp}}</ref>[[File:Mori Motonari.jpg|180px|thumb|Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Motonari, who was a powerful feudal lord in the [[Sengoku period]] and laid the foundation for the Mōri clan of the [[Chōshū Domain]].
=== History === In 1600 (Keichō 5), following the defeat in the [[Battle of Sekigahara]], Mōri Terumoto moved from [[Aki Province]] to [[Nagato Province]]. He enshrined his grandfather Motonari's spirit at Kasuga Shrine in the village of Ekimukai, Hagi. In 1762 (Hōreki 12), the Chōshū domain lord Mōri Shigetoshi rebuilt the local shrine in the Ni-no-Maru of [[Hagi Castle]] and moved Motonari's spirit there, naming it Shōrei Shrine. In 1770 (Meiwa 7), the shrine was renamed Gyōtoku Shrine and Motonari was enshrined as Gyōtoku Daimyōjin.
In 1869 (Meiji 2), Emperor Meiji granted Motonari the divine title of "Toyosaka" due to his reverence for the imperial court. The shrine was moved to the precincts of Taga Shrine in Yamaguchi City and renamed Toyosaka Shrine. The current shrine buildings were constructed in 1872 (Meiji 4), and the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9). In 1882 (Meiji 15), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main shrine building is a three-bay nagare-zukuri style structure. It has a cross-shaped flat layout with an extended roof at the front. In 1997 (Heisei 9), a monument with the inscription "One Heart with Millions" (百万一心) was erected in the grounds, based on the story of Motonari burying a stone with this inscription during the construction of Yoshida-Kōriyama Castle.
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties *Hanging scroll portrait of Mōri Motonari (Designated on June 20, 1969) [[File:Mori Motonari painting at Toyosaka Shrine.jpg|right|thumb|Full portrait of Motonari.]]
== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
=== deity === The shrine enshrines Mōri Takachika, the last lord of the Chōshū Domain and a key figure in the [[Meiji Restoration]]. It also enshrines his adopted son, Mōri Motonaga.
=== History === In 1873 (Meiji 6), volunteers built a shrine to enshrine Takachika in the grounds of Toyosaka Shrine. The shrine was named Chūsei Shrine after Takachika's posthumous name. In 1874 (Meiji 7), the shrine was renamed Noda Shrine, taking its name from the local area. It was designated a prefectural shrine in 1876 (Meiji 9) and relocated to its current site in 1886 (Meiji 19). In 1915 (Taishō 4), it was elevated to the Rank of special government shrine.
Motonaga was enshrined in 1898 (Meiji 31) as Mōri Motonaga Kō in the subsidiary shrine Hōgien Shrine within the Noda Shrine grounds. In 1936 (Shōwa 11), he was moved to the main hall.
=== Buildings and Grounds === The main buildings are similar in style and layout to those of Toyosaka Shrine. The Noh theater stage within the grounds was donated by Mōri Motonaga’s son, Mōri Motoharu, in 1936 (Shōwa 11). It was designated as a Tangible Cultural Property by Yamaguchi City in 1991 (Heisei 3).
=== Cultural Properties === ;National Important Cultural Properties * Sword (Tachi) signed by Suketomo, with ceremonial mountings (Designated on September 3, 1912) ;Yamaguchi City Tangible Cultural Properties * Noh Theater Stage and auxiliary rooms (Designated on April 23, 1991)
==Overview==
[[File:Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Trees and Torii.jpg|thumb|300px|Pathway Entrance]] '''Toyosaka Shrine''' (豊榮神社) and '''Noda Shrine''' (野田神社) are adjacent Shinto shrines located in [[Yamaguchi City]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], Japan. Although the shrines are adjacent, with Toyosaka Shrine to the east and Noda Shrine to the west, they are separate religious corporations. They each have their own main halls, worship halls, and corridors, but they share some facilities such as the office. Due to their close relationship, they are explained together here.
Both shrines were formerly designated as special government shrines (別格官幣社) and are currently listed as special shrines under the Association of Shinto Shrines (神社本庁). The Yamaguchi Prefecture Shrine Office is also located on the adjacent grounds. These shrines were built in the late Edo period to early Meiji period to enshrine the founders of the Han (feudal domains).
== Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[JR West]] [[Yamaguchi Line]]'s [[Kami-Yamaguchi Station]].
== Nearby == * [[Noda Gakuen Junior and Senior High School]] * [[Yamaguchi Saikōtei]] * [[Rurikō-ji]] * [[Dōshū-ji]], Nomura Art Museum * [[Jōei-ji]] * [[Yamaguchi Gokoku Shrine]] * [[Ima Hachiman Shrine]]
== Notes == === Toyosaka Shrine in Ōda City === There is another Toyosaka Shrine in [[Ōda City]], [[Shimane Prefecture]], near the [[Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine]]. This shrine also enshrines Mōri Motonari. It was originally a temple called Chōan-ji, founded by Mōri Terumoto in 1571 (Genki 2). In 1866 (Keiō 2), the Chōshū army discovered a wooden statue of Motonari in the temple. The following year, a shrine was built within the temple grounds, and in 1870 (Meiji 3), it was named Toyosaka Shrine, and the temple was abolished.
== References == {{Reflist|30em}} * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1112175/1/1 Compiled by Niroku Koshinjo, edited by Yamada Yoneki, 'History of Loyalty Deeds: Bekkaku Kanpeishas,' pages 38–41, 92–95, Niroku Koshinjo, 1935 (National Diet Library Digital Collection)] * 'Great Dictionary of Shinto,' Volume 3, Heibonsha, 1940 * Anzu Yoshihiko and Umeda Yoshihiko, editors and supervisors, 'Shinto Dictionary,' Jinja Shinpōsha, 1968, pages 43, 46 * Shirai Eiji and Doki Masanori, editors, 'Shrine Dictionary,' Tokyo-dō Publishing, 1979, pages 252, 269–270
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat|Toyosaka-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Toyosaka Shrine}} {{Commonscat|Noda-jinja (Yamaguchi)|Noda Shrine}} * [[Shizuki-yama Shrine]]
{{Shinto-stub}}

== Noda Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|name=Noda Shrine|religious_affiliation=Shinto|image=Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|deity=[[Mori Takachika]] Mōri Motonori|consecration_year=1873| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Noda Shrine | image = [[File:Inside the Noda Shrine complex.jpg|300px]] | location = 1-1-2 Tenge, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|11|18.7|N|131|28|49.4|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark_scale:10000|name=Noda Shrine}} | deity = Mōri Takachika | Shrine Rank = Former special government shrine, special shrine | founded = 1873 (Meiji 6) | Main Building Style = Nagare-zukuri with copper roofing | festival = March 5 | Main festival = Goshōsai (May 17 for main deity, December 23 for enshrined deities) }} {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Noda Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi]], it is located next to {{ill|Toyosaka and Noda Shrines|lt=Toyosaka Shrine|ja|豊榮神社・野田神社}}.<ref name=":0" /> It is dedicated to Mōri Motonari.<ref>{{Cite web|title=YAMAGUCHI JAPAN TRAVEL GUIDE|url=https://www.visit-jy.com/|access-date=2023-04-07|website=www.visit-jy.com}}</ref>[[File:140720 Noda-jinja Yamaguchi Japan03s.jpg|thumb|Noh theater stage]]
